---
title: "Ohio River Valley Jobs Tracker"
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: cosmo
    nav-buttons: []
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup

library(ggplot2)
library(ggthemes)
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)
library(htmltools)

state_levels <- c("Ohio", "Pennsylvania", "Kentucky", "West Virginia")

state_colors <- c(
  "Ohio"          = "#00a4d9",
  "Pennsylvania"  = "#d42e12",
  "Kentucky"      = "#f2af00",
  "West Virginia" = "#3d3d3d"
)

gain_loss_colors <- c("Gain" = "#00a4d9", "Loss" = "#d42e12")

# Formats a change already expressed in thousands as a full job count with
# thousands separators, e.g. 21.6 -> "+21,600".
fmt_jobs <- function(x) {
  sprintf("%s%s", ifelse(x >= 0, "+", "-"), formatC(abs(round(x * 1000)), format = "d", big.mark = ","))
}

# ---- Dashboard period settings ----------------------------------------------
# Update chart_month_num / chart_year here each month; every date label and
# title on every tab is derived from these two values, plus the automatically
# computed prior month and YTD baseline year below.

chart_month_num <- 5
chart_year      <- 2026

chart_month <- month.name[chart_month_num]

if (chart_month_num == 1) {
  prior_month_num <- 12
  prior_year       <- chart_year - 1
} else {
  prior_month_num <- chart_month_num - 1
  prior_year       <- chart_year
}
prior_month <- month.name[prior_month_num]

# Year-to-date baseline is always December of the prior calendar year.
baseline_year <- chart_year - 1

sector_data  <- read.csv("state_sector_data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
summary_data <- read.csv("state_net_change_summary.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
rolling_data <- read.csv("state_sector_rolling_data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
ytd_data     <- read.csv("state_sector_ytd_data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
growth_vs_national <- read.csv("state_ytd_growth_vs_national.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

sector_data$state <- factor(sector_data$state, levels = state_levels)
ytd_data$state     <- factor(ytd_data$state, levels = state_levels)

# Order sectors by total (4-state) change, ascending.
sector_order <- sector_data %>%
  group_by(sector) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(change, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(total) %>%
  pull(sector)
sector_data$sector <- factor(sector_data$sector, levels = sector_order)

# Same treatment for YTD change, ordered by its own 4-state total (independent
# of the current-month order above, since a sector can rank differently YTD).
ytd_sector_order <- ytd_data %>%
  group_by(sector) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(change, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(total) %>%
  pull(sector)
ytd_data$sector <- factor(ytd_data$sector, levels = ytd_sector_order)

# Region-wide rolling average totals, and the sector order they define.
# This same order (by region-wide rolling average, ascending) is reused for
# every rolling-average chart - region and each individual state - so the
# sectors line up row-for-row across all 5 charts.
region_rolling_totals <- rolling_data %>%
  group_by(sector, level) %>%
  summarise(rolling_avg = sum(rolling_avg, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(rolling_avg)

rolling_sector_order <- region_rolling_totals$sector

region_rolling_totals$sector <- factor(region_rolling_totals$sector, levels = rolling_sector_order)
region_rolling_totals$direction <- ifelse(region_rolling_totals$rolling_avg >= 0, "Gain", "Loss")

# Caps a plotly widget's width so bar charts don't stretch thin across wide
# monitors - width still shrinks to fit narrower screens, it just stops
# growing past max_width_px.
constrain_width <- function(widget, max_width_px) {
  div(
    style = sprintf("max-width: %dpx; width: 100%%; margin: 0 auto;", max_width_px),
    widget
  )
}

make_state_rolling_plot <- function(state_name) {
  df <- rolling_data %>% filter(state == state_name)
  df$sector <- factor(df$sector, levels = rolling_sector_order)
  df$direction <- ifelse(df$rolling_avg >= 0, "Gain", "Loss")

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = sector, y = rolling_avg, fill = direction,
                       text = sprintf("%s<br>%+.2f thousand/month", sector, rolling_avg))) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "white") +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = gain_loss_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Thousands / month") +
    theme_economist(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))

  plot_widget <- ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text") %>%
    layout(margin = list(l = 150, t = 20, b = 50), autosize = TRUE) %>%
    config(responsive = TRUE)

  constrain_width(plot_widget, 650)
}
```

# Current Month

```{r}
# Single-month net change per state + region total (see
# state_net_change_analysis.R). Not a rolling average.
net_change_lookup <- setNames(summary_data$net_change, summary_data$state)
wv_net_change_note <- summary_data$note[summary_data$state == "West Virginia"]
```

## Row {height="18%"}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Ohio, net change"
list(icon = if (net_change_lookup["Ohio"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (net_change_lookup["Ohio"] >= 0) "success" else "danger",
     value = fmt_jobs(net_change_lookup["Ohio"]))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Pennsylvania, net change"
list(icon = if (net_change_lookup["Pennsylvania"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (net_change_lookup["Pennsylvania"] >= 0) "success" else "danger",
     value = fmt_jobs(net_change_lookup["Pennsylvania"]))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Kentucky, net change"
list(icon = if (net_change_lookup["Kentucky"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (net_change_lookup["Kentucky"] >= 0) "success" else "danger",
     value = fmt_jobs(net_change_lookup["Kentucky"]))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "West Virginia, net change"
list(icon = if (net_change_lookup["West Virginia"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (net_change_lookup["West Virginia"] >= 0) "success" else "danger",
     value = fmt_jobs(net_change_lookup["West Virginia"]))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Region total, net change"
list(icon = if (net_change_lookup["Region Total"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (net_change_lookup["Region Total"] >= 0) "success" else "danger",
     value = fmt_jobs(net_change_lookup["Region Total"]))
```

## Row

```{r}
#| title: !expr sprintf("%s %d Employment Change by Industry - Ohio, Pennsylvania, Kentucky, West Virginia", chart_month, chart_year)

p_segmented <- ggplot(sector_data, aes(
    x = sector, y = change, fill = state,
    text = sprintf("%s<br>%s: %+.1f thousand", sector, state, change)
  )) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, position = "stack") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "white") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = state_colors, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Employment change (thousands), seasonally adjusted") +
  theme_economist(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top", plot.margin = margin(t = 30, r = 20, b = 10, l = 10))

segmented_plot <- ggplotly(p_segmented, tooltip = "text") %>%
  layout(legend = list(orientation = "h", x = 0.15, y = 1.08),
         margin = list(l = 160, t = 40), autosize = TRUE) %>%
  config(responsive = TRUE)

tagList(
  segmented_plot,
  tags$p(
    style = "font-size: 0.8em; color: #555; margin-top: 8px;",
    sprintf(
      "Note: figures above are single-month change (%s %d vs. %s %d), not a rolling average. %s",
      chart_month, chart_year, prior_month, prior_year, wv_net_change_note
    )
  )
)
```

# 3-Month Rolling Avg. by Industry

## Row {height="45%"}

```{r}
#| title: "Region (Ohio + Pennsylvania + Kentucky + West Virginia combined)"

p_region <- ggplot(region_rolling_totals, aes(
    x = sector, y = rolling_avg, fill = direction,
    text = sprintf("%s<br>%+.2f thousand/month", sector, rolling_avg)
  )) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "white") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = gain_loss_colors) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Avg. monthly employment change (thousands)") +
  theme_economist(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))

region_plot <- ggplotly(p_region, tooltip = "text") %>%
  layout(margin = list(l = 170, t = 20, b = 50), autosize = TRUE) %>%
  config(responsive = TRUE)

constrain_width(region_plot, 1000)
```

## Row {height="55%"}

### Column

```{r}
#| title: "Ohio"
make_state_rolling_plot("Ohio")
```

### Column

```{r}
#| title: "Pennsylvania"
make_state_rolling_plot("Pennsylvania")
```

## Row {height="55%"}

### Column

```{r}
#| title: "Kentucky"
make_state_rolling_plot("Kentucky")
```

### Column

```{r}
#| title: "West Virginia"
make_state_rolling_plot("West Virginia")
```

# Year to Date

```{r}
# Per-state YTD total (supersectors only, subsectors excluded to avoid
# double-counting), formatted as actual job counts (not thousands).
ytd_state_totals <- ytd_data %>%
  filter(level == "supersector") %>%
  group_by(state) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(change, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

ytd_totals_lookup <- setNames(ytd_state_totals$total, as.character(ytd_state_totals$state))

# YTD growth rate relative to the national rate, in percentage points.
# Positive = state growing faster than the nation YTD; negative = slower.
fmt_pp <- function(x) sprintf("%+.2f pp", x)

relative_growth_lookup <- setNames(growth_vs_national$relative_growth_pp,
                                    as.character(growth_vs_national$state))
```

## Row {height="18%"}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Ohio, YTD job growth"
list(icon = if (ytd_totals_lookup["Ohio"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (ytd_totals_lookup["Ohio"] >= 0) "primary" else "danger",
     value = fmt_jobs(ytd_totals_lookup["Ohio"]))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Pennsylvania, YTD job growth"
list(icon = if (ytd_totals_lookup["Pennsylvania"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (ytd_totals_lookup["Pennsylvania"] >= 0) "primary" else "danger",
     value = fmt_jobs(ytd_totals_lookup["Pennsylvania"]))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Kentucky, YTD job growth"
list(icon = if (ytd_totals_lookup["Kentucky"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (ytd_totals_lookup["Kentucky"] >= 0) "primary" else "danger",
     value = fmt_jobs(ytd_totals_lookup["Kentucky"]))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "West Virginia, YTD job growth"
list(icon = if (ytd_totals_lookup["West Virginia"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (ytd_totals_lookup["West Virginia"] >= 0) "primary" else "danger",
     value = fmt_jobs(ytd_totals_lookup["West Virginia"]))
```

## Row {height="18%"}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Ohio, YTD growth vs. U.S."
list(icon = if (relative_growth_lookup["Ohio"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (relative_growth_lookup["Ohio"] >= 0) "success" else "danger",
     value = fmt_pp(relative_growth_lookup["Ohio"]))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Pennsylvania, YTD growth vs. U.S."
list(icon = if (relative_growth_lookup["Pennsylvania"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (relative_growth_lookup["Pennsylvania"] >= 0) "success" else "danger",
     value = fmt_pp(relative_growth_lookup["Pennsylvania"]))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Kentucky, YTD growth vs. U.S."
list(icon = if (relative_growth_lookup["Kentucky"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (relative_growth_lookup["Kentucky"] >= 0) "success" else "danger",
     value = fmt_pp(relative_growth_lookup["Kentucky"]))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "West Virginia, YTD growth vs. U.S."
list(icon = if (relative_growth_lookup["West Virginia"] >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
     color = if (relative_growth_lookup["West Virginia"] >= 0) "success" else "danger",
     value = fmt_pp(relative_growth_lookup["West Virginia"]))
```

## Row

```{r}
#| title: !expr sprintf("Year-to-Date Employment Change by Industry - December %d to %s %d", baseline_year, chart_month, chart_year)

p_ytd <- ggplot(ytd_data, aes(
    x = sector, y = change, fill = state,
    text = sprintf("%s<br>%s: %+.1f thousand", sector, state, change)
  )) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, position = "stack") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, color = "white") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = state_colors, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cumulative employment change (thousands), seasonally adjusted") +
  theme_economist(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top", plot.margin = margin(t = 30, r = 20, b = 10, l = 10))

ytd_plot <- ggplotly(p_ytd, tooltip = "text") %>%
  layout(legend = list(orientation = "h", x = 0.15, y = 1.08),
         margin = list(l = 160, t = 40), autosize = TRUE) %>%
  config(responsive = TRUE)

tagList(
  ytd_plot,
  tags$p(
    style = "font-size: 0.8em; color: #555; margin-top: 8px;",
    sprintf(
      paste(
        "Note: cumulative change is measured from December %d (the standard year-to-date baseline)",
        "through %s %d. West Virginia's Government total - and its outsized YTD growth vs. U.S. figure",
        "above - still includes the same April-to-May seasonal-adjustment artifact discussed on the",
        "Current Month tab, so both should be read with that in mind."
      ),
      baseline_year, chart_month, chart_year
    )
  )
)
```
